headline: Youth Unemployment Rate Doubles National Average at 10.8%
context: DOSM reported 10.8% unemployment among 15-24 year olds in Q4 2025, compared to 5.2% nationally.

cards.text: Youth unemployment at 10.8% is double the national rate of 5.2% in Q4 2025
cards.sub: 388,000 young Malaysians are actively seeking but unable to find work

cards.h: Gig economy absorbs 42% of working youth but with no EPF or SOCSO coverage
cards.s: Underemployment disguises the true extent of youth economic precarity
cards.d: Gig economy absorbs 42% of working youth but with no EPF or SOCSO coverage Underemployment disguises the true extent of youth economic precarity

cards.h: Minimum wage of RM1,500 has not changed since May 2022 despite 12% cumulative inflation
cards.s: Real purchasing power of entry-level wages continues to erode
cards.d: Minimum wage of RM1,500 has not changed since May 2022 despite 12% cumulative inflation Real purchasing power of entry-level wages continues to erode

cards.h: ILO reports Malaysia has highest youth NEET rate in ASEAN at 11.3% for 2025
cards.s: Not in employment education or training is a leading indicator of social exclusion
cards.d: ILO reports Malaysia has highest youth NEET rate in ASEAN at 11.3% for 2025 Not in employment education or training is a leading indicator of social exclusion

cards.text: A generation locked out of stable work will not build the families and businesses Malaysia needs
Like building a pipeline that feeds into a reservoir with no outlet. The graduates accumulate, but the jobs to absorb them do not exist at the same rate.
cards.sub: Education expansion without proportional job creation creates a bottleneck that hits young workers hardest

cards.text: Should Malaysia introduce a youth employment guarantee program for under-25s
